T2.DMM 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Getting Started with the Concept Management Model Package

    Getting Started with the Concept Management Model Package

    concept_management_model.Rmd

    Overview

    The Concept Management Model R package provides a comprehensive data model and functionality for processing Real-World Data (RWD) in a Common Data Model (CDM) format. This vignette demonstrates a complete workflow for loading, transforming, and analyzing CDM data.

    Installation 

    # Install the package (example installation method)
devtools::install_github("UMC-Utrecht-RWE/T2-DataManagementModel")
    library(T2.DMM)
library(data.table)
#> Warning: package 'data.table' was built under R version 4.4.3
library(DBI)
#> Warning: package 'DBI' was built under R version 4.4.3
library(duckdb)
#> Warning: package 'duckdb' was built under R version 4.4.3

    Workflow Overview

    The T2-DataManagementModel workflow consists of 3 main processes:

    1. Set Database: Load and clean raw CDM data
    2. Process Codelist: Extract and standardize codes from the data
    3. Apply Codelist: Harmonize codes and create standardized concepts
    Raw Data (CSV/Parquet)
    ↓
[PROCESS 1: SET DATABASE]
    ├─ Load data into database
    ├─ Remove missing person IDs (optional)
    ├─ Remove duplicates (optional)
    └─ Add unique identifiers  
    ↓
Cleaned CDM Database
    ↓
[PROCESS 2: PROCESS CODELIST]
    ├─ Extract unique codes from database
    ├─ Create DAP-specific codelist
    ├─ Prepare concept map (wrangling)
    └─ Merge DAP-specific codelist and concept map
    ↓
Harmonized Codelist
    ↓
[PROCESS 3: APPLY CODELIST]
    ├─ Load DAP-specific codelist + concept map
    ├─ Initialize concept table
    └─ Apply codelist to create concepts
    ↓
Harmonized Concept Table

    PROCESS 1: SET DATABASE

    This process loads raw data files, applies data quality transformations, and prepares the database for analysis.

    Step 1.1: Initialize Database Connection and Loader

    Begin by creating a DatabaseLoader object, which manages the database connection, configuration, and data loading process.

      db_path <- tempfile(pattern = "cdm", fileext = ".duckdb")
    # Initialize the DatabaseLoader
loader <- DatabaseLoader$new(
  db_path = db_path,
  data_instance = file.path(getwd(),"data/"),
  config_path = file.path(getwd(),"data/set_db.json"),
  cdm_metadata = file.path(getwd(),"data/CDM_metadata.rds")
)

    Parameters: - db_path: Path to the DuckDB database file (created if doesn’t exist) - data_instance: Path to the directory containing CSV or Parquet files - config_path: Path to JSON configuration file specifying operations and settings - cdm_metadata: Path to RDS file containing CDM variable metadata

    Step 1.2: Load CSV/Parquet Files into Database

    Use the set_database() method to load your CDM data from CSV or Parquet files.

    # Load the data into the database
loader$set_database()
#> Setting up the database
#> [load_db]: Reading for table: PERSONS
#> [load_db]: Using csv format for table: PERSONS
#> [load_db]: Finished reading PERSONS (csv)
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: person_id
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: day_of_birth
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: month_of_birth
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: year_of_birth
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: day_of_death
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: month_of_death
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: year_of_death
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: sex_at_instance_creation
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: race
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: country_of_birth
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: quality
#> [load_db]: Reading for table: VACCINES
#> [load_db]: Using csv format for table: VACCINES
#> [load_db]: Finished reading VACCINES (csv)
#> [load_db]: The following columns are identified with date format: vx_record_date,vx_admin_date
#> Converting: vx_record_date
#>       Direct conversion failed. Attempting to fix with STRPTIME.
#>       Column successfully converted to DATE after fixing format.
#> Converting: vx_admin_date
#>       Direct conversion failed. Attempting to fix with STRPTIME.
#>       Column successfully converted to DATE after fixing format.
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: person_id
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: vx_dose
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: vx_manufacturer
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: vx_atc
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: vx_type
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: vx_text
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: origin_of_vx_record
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: meaning_of_vx_record
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: vx_lot_num
#> [load_db]: Reading for table: OBSERVATION_PERIODS
#> [load_db]: No files found for OBSERVATION_PERIODS, in: /Users/acidroyo/Documents/GitHub/T2-DataManagementModel/vignettes/data/
#> [load_db]: Reading for table: MEDICAL_OBSERVATIONS
#> [load_db]: No files found for MEDICAL_OBSERVATIONS, in: /Users/acidroyo/Documents/GitHub/T2-DataManagementModel/vignettes/data/
#> [load_db]: Reading for table: MEDICINES
#> [load_db]: No files found for MEDICINES, in: /Users/acidroyo/Documents/GitHub/T2-DataManagementModel/vignettes/data/
#> [load_db]: Reading for table: EVENTS
#> [load_db]: Using csv format for table: EVENTS
#> [load_db]: Finished reading EVENTS (csv)
#> [load_db]: The following columns are identified with date format: start_date_record,end_date_record
#> Converting: start_date_record
#>       Direct conversion failed. Attempting to fix with STRPTIME.
#>       Column successfully converted to DATE after fixing format.
#> Converting: end_date_record
#>       Direct conversion failed. Attempting to fix with STRPTIME.
#>       Column successfully converted to DATE after fixing format.
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: person_id
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: event_code
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: event_record_vocabulary
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: text_linked_to_event_code
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: event_free_text
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: present_on_admission
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: laterality_of_event
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: meaning_of_event
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: origin_of_event
#>   Cleaning character column from empty spaces: visit_occurrence_id
#> [load_db]: Reading for table: SURVEY_OBSERVATIONS
#> [load_db]: No files found for SURVEY_OBSERVATIONS, in: /Users/acidroyo/Documents/GitHub/T2-DataManagementModel/vignettes/data/
#> [load_db]: Reading for table: SURVEY_ID
#> [load_db]: No files found for SURVEY_ID, in: /Users/acidroyo/Documents/GitHub/T2-DataManagementModel/vignettes/data/
#> [load_db]: Reading for table: VISIT_OCCURRENCE
#> [load_db]: No files found for VISIT_OCCURRENCE, in: /Users/acidroyo/Documents/GitHub/T2-DataManagementModel/vignettes/data/

    This method: - Reads all files from data_instance directory - Automatically detects file format (CSV, Parquet, or auto-detect) - Creates database tables matching CDM structure - Applies any extension names specified in configuration

    Configuration Example:

    The config.json should include table names and loading parameters:

    {
    "data_model": "ConcePTION",
    "file_format" : "csv",
    "operations_path": "R",
    "operations": {
      "DuplicateRemover": true,
      "MissingRemover": true,
      "UniqueIdGenerator": true,
      "ReportGenerator": true
    },
    "duplicate_remover": {
      "save_path": "intermediate_data_file",
      "add_postfix": null,
      "save_deleted": true,
      "cdm_tables_columns": {
        "PERSONS": ["person_id", "country_of_birth"],
        "VACCINES": ["person_id", "vx_manufacturer"]
      },
      "to_view": false
    },
    "missing_remover": {
      "columns": {
        "PERSONS": ["person_id"],
        "VACCINES": ["person_id"],
        "OBSERVATION_PERIODS": ["person_id"],
        "MEDICAL_OBSERVATIONS": ["person_id"],
        "MEDICINES": ["person_id"],
        "EVENTS": ["person_id"],
        "SURVEY_OBSERVATIONS": ["person_id"],
        "SURVEY_ID": ["person_id"],
        "VISIT_OCCURRENCE": ["person_id"]
      },
      "to_view": false
    },
    "cdm_tables_names": [
      "PERSONS",
      "VACCINES",
      "OBSERVATION_PERIODS",
      "MEDICAL_OBSERVATIONS",
      "MEDICINES",
      "EVENTS",
      "SURVEY_OBSERVATIONS",
      "SURVEY_ID",
      "VISIT_OCCURRENCE"
    ],
    "unique_id_generator":{
      "instance_name": "",
      "to_view": false
    },
    "report_generator": {
      "report_path": ".",
      "report_name": "count_rows_origin.fst"
    }
  }

    Step 1.3: Execute Transformations via run_db_ops()

    All data quality transformations are applied through the run_db_ops() method, which executes database operations defined in your configuration file sequentially.

    # Execute all configured operations
loader$run_db_ops()
#> Loading operation: DuplicateRemover
#> Loading operation: MissingRemover
#> Loading operation: UniqueIdGenerator
#> Loading operation: ReportGenerator
#> Running class: DuplicateRemover
#> Removing duplicates from tables.
#> [delete_duplicates_origin] Deleting records from: PERSONS
#> [delete_duplicates_origin] Number of record deleted: 0
#> [delete_duplicates_origin] Deleting records from: VACCINES
#> [delete_duplicates_origin] Number of record deleted: 0
#> Duplicates removed.
#> Running class: MissingRemover
#> Removing missing values from tables.
#> [clean_missing_values] PERSONS
#>   -> Table PERSONS overwritten with cleaned version
#> [clean_missing_values] VACCINES
#>   -> Table VACCINES overwritten with cleaned version
#> Table OBSERVATION_PERIODS does not exist
#> Table MEDICAL_OBSERVATIONS does not exist
#> Table MEDICINES does not exist
#> [clean_missing_values] EVENTS
#>   -> Table EVENTS overwritten with cleaned version
#> Table SURVEY_OBSERVATIONS does not exist
#> Table SURVEY_ID does not exist
#> Table VISIT_OCCURRENCE does not exist
#> Missing values removed.
#> Running class: UniqueIdGenerator
#> [CreateUniqueIDCDM] Can not create unique IDs on the following CDM table because they do not exist in the database
#> OBSERVATION_PERIODSMEDICAL_OBSERVATIONSMEDICINESSURVEY_OBSERVATIONSSURVEY_IDVISIT_OCCURRENCE
#> [CreateUniqueIDCDM] Unique ID create for table: main.PERSONS
#> [CreateUniqueIDCDM] Unique ID create for table: main.VACCINES
#> [CreateUniqueIDCDM] Unique ID create for table: main.EVENTS
#> Unique IDs created.
#> Running class: ReportGenerator
#> Getting tables from the database
#> Tables found: EVENTS, PERSONS, VACCINES
#> Generated query: SELECT 'EVENTS' AS name, (SELECT COUNT(1) FROM EVENTS) AS row_count UNION ALL SELECT 'PERSONS' AS name, (SELECT COUNT(1) FROM PERSONS) AS row_count UNION ALL SELECT 'VACCINES' AS name, (SELECT COUNT(1) FROM VACCINES) AS row_count
#> Generating reports for: count_rows_origin.fst
#> R6 classes in T2.DMM package: CSVReportGenerator, DatabaseLoader, DatabaseOperation, DuplicateRemover, FSTReportGenerator, MissingRemover, ReportGenerator, UniqueIdGenerator
#> Subclasses of ReportGenerator: CSVReportGenerator, FSTReportGenerator
#> Extensions for CSVReportGenerator: csv
#> Extensions for FSTReportGenerator: fst
#> Found subclass FSTReportGenerator for extension: count_rows_origin.fst
#> Using subclass: FSTReportGenerator
#> Report saved successfully.
#>           used (Mb) gc trigger  (Mb) limit (Mb) max used (Mb)
#> Ncells 1208177 64.6    2532192 135.3         NA  1478621 79.0
#> Vcells 2173659 16.6    8388608  64.0      16384  3915153 29.9

    Transformation Operations

    MissingRemover: Remove Records with Missing Person IDs

    The MissingRemover operation identifies and removes rows with missing or invalid values in critical columns (like person_id). This ensures data integrity by preventing orphaned records.

    What it does: - Removes rows where specified columns are NULL, empty string, or ‘NA’ - Configurable per table - Can optionally create views instead of deleting (with to_view: true) - Reports how many rows were removed from each table

    Configuration:

    {
  "missing_remover": {
    "columns": {
      "EVENTS": ["person_id", "event_code"],
      "VACCINES": ["person_id"],
      "MEDICINES": ["person_id"]
    },
    "to_view": false
  }
}
    DuplicateRemover: Remove Duplicate Records

    The DuplicateRemover operation identifies and removes duplicate records based on specified column combinations.

    What it does: - Identifies duplicate rows within each table - Keeps first occurrence, removes others - "*" means use all columns for duplicate detection - Can optionally save deleted records as CSV files for auditing - Improves data quality and reduces analysis bias

    Configuration:

    {
  "duplicate_remover": {
    "scheme": {
      "EVENTS": "*",
      "VACCINES": ["person_id", "vx_record_date"],
      "MEDICINES": ["person_id", "medicine_code"]
    },
    "save_deleted": false,
    "save_path": "./deleted_records"
  }
}
    UniqueIdGenerator: Add Unique Identifiers

    The UniqueIdGenerator operation creates unique identifiers for all records, enabling audit trails and tracking data through the pipeline.

    What it does: - Generates unique IDs for each record in specified tables - Uses instance name as suffix (e.g., “record_id_T2DMM”) - Creates a column for tracking records through processing - Useful for linking back to original records

    Configuration:

    {
  "unique_id_generator": {
    "instance_name": "_T2DMM"
  }
}
    ReportGenerator: Count Records per Table

    The ReportGenerator operation counts records in each table after all transformations are complete.

    Configuration:

    {
  "report_generator": {
    "report_name": "count_rows_origin.csv",
    "report_path": "./"
  }
}

    Output Example:

      table_name record_count
1     PERSONS         1000
2      EVENTS         5250
3     VACCINES         3100
4    MEDICINES         8500

    PROCESS 2: PROCESS CODELIST

    This process extracts codes from your cleaned database and standardizes them against reference codelists for consistent mapping across different coding systems.

    Step 2.1: Extract Unique Codes with get_unique_codelist()

    Extract unique codes from your database using get_unique_codelist(). This function identifies all distinct codes in specified columns and provides counts for data quality assessment.

    # Reconnect to database if needed
db_connection <- DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), db_path)

# Define which columns to extract as codelists
column_info_list <- list(
  list(
    source_column = c("event_code", "event_record_vocabulary"),
    alias_name = c("code", "coding_system")
  )
)

# Extract unique codelists
dap_codes <- get_unique_codelist(
  db_connection = db_connection,
  column_info_list = column_info_list,
  tb_name = "EVENTS"
)

dap_codes <- as.data.table(dap_codes[[1]])
# View the results
DT::datatable(dap_codes,options = list(scrollX = TRUE))

    Parameters: - source_column: Column name(s) in database to extract - alias_name: New name(s) for the column(s) in output - tb_name: Table name to extract from - COUNT: Automatically added - frequency of each code

    Step 2.2: Create DAP-Specific Codelist with create_dap_specific_codelist()

    Create a data analysis-specific codelist by matching codes from your data against a reference codelist. This is essential for standardizing codes across different coding systems (ICD-10, SNOMED CT, ATC, etc.).

    # Load your reference codelist
# This typically contains all standard codes and their mappings
reference_codelist <- data.table::fread(file.path(getwd(),"data/code_list.csv"))

# Create a standardized codelist with intelligent matching
dap_specific_codelist <- create_dap_specific_codelist(
  dap_codes = dap_codes,
  codelist = reference_codelist,
  start_with_codingsystems = c(
    "ICD10CM", "ICD10", "ICD10DA", "ICD9CM", "MTHICD9",
    "ICPC", "ICPC2P", "ICPC2EENG", "ATC"
  )
)
#> -----> The following coding systems will be searched 
#>                  with a start with approach: ICD10CM, ICD10, ICD10DA, ICD9CM, MTHICD9, ICPC, ICPC2P, ICPC2EENG, ATC
#> Column 'priority' not found. Assigning default value 1.
#> [SetCodesheets] Max length of 
#>                    code from the DAP is : 5

# View results
DT::datatable(dap_specific_codelist,options = list(scrollX = TRUE))

    How create_dap_specific_codelist Works:

    1. Input Validation: Checks that input data tables contain required columns
    2. Code Standardization: Marks source codes to avoid collisions during merging
    3. System Classification: Separates codes by matching strategy:
      • Hierarchical Systems (ICD-10, ATC): Use “start-with” prefix matching
      • Exact Match Systems (PRODCODEID): Use exact code matching
    4. Intelligent Matching:
      • For prefix-based systems, tries multiple prefix lengths to find matches
      • Example: “I10” matches reference code “I10.9”

    This process transforms your codelist into a format suitable for CDM application and applies harmonized codes to create standardized concepts.

    Step 2.3: Prepare Codelist with wrangling_codelist() 

    # Transform to long format the codelist to comply with the apply_codelist() function
dap_specific_codelist <- dap_specific_codelist[match_status == "MATCHED"]
dap_specific_codelist[, keep_value_column_name := NA_character_ ]
dap_specific_codelist[, keep_date_column_name := "start_date_record" ]

long_format_codelist <- wrangling_codelist(
    codelist = dap_specific_codelist,
    code_column_name = "event_code",
    codingsystem_column_name = "event_record_vocabulary"
  )

# View results
DT::datatable(long_format_codelist,options = list(scrollX = TRUE))

    Step 2.4: Prepare Concept Map with wrangling_concept_map()

    Transform your DAP-specific concept map from wide format to long format suitable for applying to the CDM.

    # Load your concept map in RWE BRIDGE metadata format
# Expected columns: concept_id, cdm_table_name, 
#                   column_name_*, expected_value_*,
#                   keep_value_column_name, keep_date_column_name

dap_specific_concept_map <- data.table::fread(file.path(getwd(),"data/dap_specific_concept_map.csv"))

# Transform to long format
long_format_concept_map <- wrangling_concept_map(dap_specific_concept_map)

    Input Format (Wide):

    The concept map should be in RWE BRIDGE format with columns like: - concept_id: Target concept identifier - cdm_table_name: CDM table to map from - column_name_1, column_name_2: CDM columns to match - expected_value_1, expected_value_2: Values to match against - keep_value_column_name: Column whose value to retain - keep_date_column_name: Column for the date value

    DT::datatable(dap_specific_concept_map,options = list(scrollX = TRUE))

    Output Format (Long):

    The function returns a normalized long format:

    DT::datatable(long_format_concept_map,options = list(scrollX = TRUE))

    What wrangling_concept_map Does:

    • Assigns unique id_set identifier to each mapping
    • Converts wide format (column_name_*, expected_value_*) to long format
    • Generates order_index for hierarchical processing (parent=1, children>1)
    • Filters out NA values in cdm_column

    PROCESS 3: APPLY CODELIST

    Step 3.1: Initialize Concept Table with initialize_concept_table()

    Create the target concept_table that will store harmonized concepts:

    # Initialize the concept table as a database table
initialize_concept_table(
  con = db_connection,
  type_table = "table",  # or "view" for Parquet-backed table
  partition = TRUE,
  add_id_set = TRUE
)

# Alternatively, initialize as a view reading from Parquet files
initialize_concept_table(
  con = db_connection,
  type_table = "view",
  path_parquets = "/path/to/parquet/files",
  partition = TRUE,
  add_id_set = TRUE
)

    Parameters:

    • type_table: Either "table" (persistent SQL table) or "view" (Parquet-backed view)
    • path_parquets: Required if type_table = "view" - directory containing Parquet files
    • partition: If TRUE, enables Hive partitioning for Parquet reads
    • add_id_set: If TRUE, includes the id_set column in the schema
    • overwrite: If TRUE, drops and recreates existing concept_table

    Concept Table Schema:

    The table includes: - unique_id: Unique identifier from CDM records - ori_table: Original CDM table name - person_id: Patient identifier - concept_id: Harmonized concept - value: Clinical value (upper-cased) - date: Associated date - id_set: Mapping set identifier (optional)

    Step 3.2: Apply Codelist with apply_codelist()

    Apply the harmonized codelist to create standardized concepts:

    long_format_codelist[, id_set := paste0("codelist-",id_set)]
long_format_concept_map[, id_set := paste0("conceptmap-",id_set)]
codelist <- rbindlist(list(long_format_codelist,long_format_concept_map), use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)
# Apply the prepared codelist to the database
apply_codelist(
  db_con = db_connection,
  codelist = codelist,
  materialize = "in_database",  # or "in_parquet"
  path_parquets = NULL,  # "/path/to/save" if materialize = "in_parquet"
  keep_id_set = TRUE
)
#> [apply_codelist] Processing family 1: 1, EVENTS, 'TRUE', start_date_record
#>    Applying parent scheme(s)
#>     Processing child with cdm_column=event_record_vocabulary
#>         Order index: 2
#> [1] 2
#> [1] ",\n        t2.id_set"
#> [apply_codelist] Processing family 2: 2, VACCINES, 'TRUE', vx_admin_date
#>    Applying parent scheme(s)
#> [1] ",\n        t2.id_set"

    Parameters:

    • db_con: Valid DuckDB database connection
    • codelist: Long-format data.table from wrangling_concept_map()
    • materialize: Where to store results:
      • "in_database": Insert into concept_table in database
      • "in_parquet": Export to partitioned Parquet files
    • path_parquets: Required only if materialize = "in_parquet"
    • keep_id_set: If TRUE, keeps the mapping set identifier in results

    How apply_codelist Works:

    The function processes codelists in three phases per family:

    1. Grouping: Organizes codes into families by (cdm_table_name, keep_value_column, keep_date_column)

    2. Parent Codes (order_index = 1):

      • Uses SQL script create_concepts_1.sql
      • Matches parent codes against CDM table values
      • Creates identified_ids table with matched records

    3. Child Codes (order_index > 1):

      • Uses SQL script create_concepts_2.sql iteratively
      • Applies hierarchical filtering based on child column values
      • Refines matched record set at each level

    4. Finalization:

      • Uses SQL script create_concepts_3.sql
      • Assembles final record set with all required columns
      • Inserts into concept_table or exports to Parquet

    Output Example:

    concept_table <- dbReadTable(db_connection,"concept_table")
DT::datatable(concept_table,options = list(scrollX = TRUE))

    Key Functions Reference

    Process 1: Set Database

    Function Purpose
    DatabaseLoader$new() Initialize loader with configuration
    loader$set_database() Load CDM data from CSV/Parquet files
    loader$run_db_ops() Execute all configured transformations
    MissingRemover Remove records with missing values
    UniqueIdGenerator Add unique identifiers
    DuplicateRemover Remove duplicate records
    ReportGenerator Generate record count report

    Process 2: Process Codelist

    Function Purpose
    get_unique_codelist() Extract unique codes with frequencies
    create_dap_specific_codelist() Match and standardize codes
    wrangling_codelist() Transform codelist to format needed in apply_codelist()
    wrangling_concept_map() Transform concept map to format needed in apply_codelist()

    Process 3: Apply Codelist

    Function Purpose
    initialize_concept_table() Create concept_table for results
    apply_codelist() Apply harmonized codelists to CDM

    Troubleshooting

    Process 1: Set Database

    • Files not found: Verify data_instance path is correct and file names match table names
    • Operations not running: Check operations are enabled (true) in config.json
    • Connection errors: Ensure DuckDB is installed and file paths are writable

    Process 2: Process Codelist

    • Codelist matching issues: Verify coding systems match start_with_codingsystems parameter
    • Missing codes: Check that priority column exists in reference codelist
    • Column mismatches: Ensure alias_name matches actual column names

    Process 3: Apply Codelist

    • Wrangling errors: Verify concept map has column_name_* and expected_value_* columns
    • Table initialization fails: Check that database is writable and path_parquets exists
    • Apply_codelist errors: Ensure referenced CDM tables exist in database

    Session Information 

    sessionInfo()
#> R version 4.4.2 (2024-10-31)
#> Platform: aarch64-apple-darwin20
#> Running under: macOS 26.3.1
#> 
#> Matrix products: default
#> BLAS:   /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.4-arm64/Resources/lib/libRblas.0.dylib 
#> LAPACK: /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.4-arm64/Resources/lib/libRlapack.dylib;  LAPACK version 3.12.0
#> 
#> locale:
#> [1] en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8/C/en_US.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8
#> 
#> time zone: Europe/Amsterdam
#> tzcode source: internal
#> 
#> attached base packages:
#> [1] stats     graphics  grDevices utils     datasets  methods   base     
#> 
#> other attached packages:
#> [1] fstcore_0.10.0      duckdb_1.5.0        DBI_1.3.0          
#> [4] data.table_1.18.2.1 T2.DMM_0.1.0       
#> 
#> loaded via a namespace (and not attached):
